posture: Trust-first minimal-blast remediation of six verified defects (TC-1a/b, TC-2, TC-3, TC-4, TC-5, TC-6) in the terminal-commander Rust workspace, synthesized from the minimal-risk draft (top-scored 45/44/46 across all three judge lenses) with every judge-endorsed graft folded in and every unanimous fatal flaw eliminated. Governing axiom (grafted from trust-contract-first): NO returned error may hide a side effect; NO caller -- nor the adapter retrying on its behalf -- may be told to retry a non-idempotent op; EVERY returned state must map to caller intent. The single highest-harm item, TC-1a (a HEAD regression where a >5s client-timeout makes daemon_client.rs:191-198 blindly re-send a cloned CommandStartCombed => DOUBLE SPAWN), is killed first by the smallest correct change: gate the retry on request.is_idempotent() (F1=a), which research-gaps explicitly flagged as under-weighted. The verified footgun -- transport_unavailable_error() routes through McpError::internal_error (numeric -32603, doc comment lies) AND its remedy literally says 'retry the tool' -- is fixed in the same urgent phase by threading a mutating flag from the call site (into_mcp_error has no IpcRequest in scope, confirmed). TC-2 is then GENUINELY closed (not deferred, not reframed) by a narrow daemon-side in-flight dedup guard grafted from trust-contract-first -- but isolated in its OWN phase (Phase 2), never folded into the urgent pure-logic Phase 1, and hardened against the judge-flagged 'collapse two legitimately-distinct rapid runs' race via client-nonce keying + eviction-on-every-completion-path + a legitimate-re-run-never-blocked test. The three unanimously-rejected over-builds are NOT built: pre-spawn ack handshake (rewrites the spawn-failure contract, still double-spawns without dedup), server-honored idempotency-key protocol on RequestEnvelope (wire change, TTL/fingerprint design open), and a combined CommandWaitNext RPC (new IPC method that collides with TC-3's IPC work). TC-1b+TC-6 are co-designed in ONE wait-loop rewrite (shared body tools.rs:650-709): a wall-clock Instant deadline makes the advertised 60000ms cap honest (no slice-size change, so the load gate is untouched -- chosen over halving MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS per the judge graft) and both post-job_id error sites convert to a degraded result that is a STRICT SUPERSET of the existing run_and_watch payload (start-arm error still returns Err, no job to preserve). TC-4 surfaces bounded redacted argv_head + tag via additive serde(default) ProbeListEntry fields, fixes the into_parts tag:None fake-success path, and puts crates/cli/src/render.rs in scope so fields are not silently dropped. TC-5 hosts a real spawn round-trip in the now-async IPC handle_self_check (the surface that lies to live clients), reusing ONE cached immortal bucket, profile-gated to skip-or-assert-deny (never false-RED), with the async change propagated to BOTH the primary dispatch arm AND the dispatch_envelope/named-pipe path (judge graft), plus a NEGATIVE test proving failures>0 on real breakage. TC-3 (command_stop) is the one unavoidably multi-surface item, isolated LAST so all 5 name lists + 3 count assertions (catalogue 37->38, sorted vec, daemon_unavailable_envelope checked 36->37, fixture map live_tools 37->38/covered_live 33->34, system_discover fixture) + the 6+ IPC sync surfaces + minimal_tool_args land in one atomic reviewable PR. Seven phases (one docs-only Phase 0), each independently CI-green on BOTH OS gates, each <=10 files, ZERO new dependencies, crates/mcp/src kept free of spawn/fs/socket (kill forwarded over IPC), every new MCP tool integration-tested through the daemon IPC end, source-status discipline on every test, live machine daemon never restarted/killed (all tests use own sockets). Conventional Commits, PRs to a review branch off main, NO push/merge without explicit human approval (fix:/feat: triggers release-please). No fake success: degraded keeps job_id, self_check failures>0 on real breakage, dedup returns the real existing job_id, into_parts threads the real tag.

fork_decisions.fork: F1 (TC-1a/TC-2: stop the double-spawn) -- (a) classify-and-don't-retry mutating RPCs vs (b) server idempotency key vs (c) pre-spawn ack vs combination
fork_decisions.decision: ADOPT (a) as the PRIMARY urgent fix (Phase 1) + a narrow daemon-side in-flight dedup guard as defense-in-depth (Phase 2, SEPARATE phase). REJECT (b) the envelope idempotency-key protocol and (c) the pre-spawn ack handshake (deferred, tracked). Phase 1 is PURE LOGIC, zero daemon state: add `pub const fn is_idempotent(&self) -> bool` to IpcRequest (protocol.rs, enum at :195) as an EXHAUSTIVE match -- false for CommandStartCombed, PtyCommandStart, PtyCommandWriteStdin, RegistryUpsert/Activate/Deactivate/ImportPack, FileWatchStart/FileWatchStop, Shutdown; true for reads (Health, CommandStatus, BucketWait, RuntimeState, ProbeList, ProbeStatus, BucketEventsSince, BucketSummary, EventContext, etc.). Gate the retry at daemon_client.rs:191 from `Err(e) if e.is_transport()` to `Err(e) if e.is_transport() && request.is_idempotent()` so a transport failure on a MUTATING request returns Err WITHOUT re-send; reads keep the one-retry self-heal. Fix transport_unavailable_error (tools.rs:1798) -- VERIFIED it routes through McpError::internal_error (numeric -32603 despite its doc comment) and its remedy literally says 'retry the tool' -- by threading a mutating flag from the call site (into_mcp_error at tools.rs:1667 short-circuits transport BEFORE the code match and has NO IpcRequest in scope, so add a sibling into_mcp_error_mutating OR pass a bool, and the mutating remedy says 'the command MAY have started; call command_status/runtime_state to confirm before re-running' -- still code:daemon_unavailable, numeric code unchanged this phase). Phase 2 then GENUINELY closes TC-2 (per judge graft, not deferred): a short-TTL in-flight fingerprint on CommandRuntime, checked at the top of start_combed (command.rs ~:540, before id mint at :472), keyed PREFERENTIALLY on a client-supplied request nonce and falling back to hash(argv+cwd+tag) ONLY within a very short window (~3s) for the exact retry signature; a duplicate within the window returns the SAME job_id/bucket_id instead of spawning. CRITICAL race-avoidance (judge fatal-flaw fix): the fingerprint MUST be evicted on EVERY completion path -- normal exit (lifecycle waiter ~command.rs:687), cancel, AND the spawn-failure path (command.rs:548-560) -- and a regression test MUST prove two legitimately-distinct rapid identical runs are NOT collapsed (distinct nonces => distinct jobs) and a leaked fingerprint never blocks a legitimate re-run.
fork_decisions.rationale: research-gaps explicitly says (a) was under-weighted and is 'the smallest correct change'; all three judge lenses scored the minimal-risk draft highest precisely for keeping Phase 1 as pure classification logic. (a) eliminates the AUTOMATIC double-spawn (the HEAD regression), stays inside no-new-deps + the mcp spawn/fs/socket guard (a bool read), and is trivially testable. The judges unanimously flagged folding the stateful dedup into Phase 1 as a scope-discipline miss (it couples new daemon behavior to the PR you most want instantly mergeable) and flagged a naive fingerprint as a NEW correctness bug (silently collapsing two distinct rapid runs); both are resolved by (1) a separate phase and (2) nonce-preferred keying with eviction-everywhere + the never-collapse/never-block test. (b) is a wire/protocol change with open TTL/fingerprint design the researchers left unresolved; (c) rewrites the spawn-failure contract (command.rs:548-560 must flip an already-acked job to Failed) and research-gaps confirms it STILL double-spawns under blind retry -- redundant once (a)+dedup exist. Both deferred with tracked BACKLOG + RISK_REGISTER rows.
fork_decisions.decision_short: F1=(a) gate retry on is_idempotent() + fix -32603/'retry' remedy (Phase 1, pure logic); + narrow nonce-keyed in-flight dedup closing TC-2 (Phase 2, separate); defer (b) envelope key and (c) pre-spawn ack with tracked rows

fork_decisions.fork: F2 (TC-1b recovery contract / partial-result shape once job_id is known)
fork_decisions.decision: Once run_and_watch holds a job_id (after the start RPC at tools.rs:630-643 succeeds), convert ANY subsequent in-loop RPC error (CommandStatus at :665, BucketWait at :703) into a DEGRADED success-shaped (isError:false) result that is a STRICT SUPERSET of the existing run_and_watch payload (tools.rs:724-736), so no existing reader breaks: {job_id, bucket_id, state (last known, default Running), exit_code (last known), signals (collected so far), signal_count, cursor (surfaced so the caller can resume the subscription from the exact position), receipt:null, complete:false, wait_exhausted:true, degraded:true, recover_hint:'IPC error mid-wait; the job is still tracked -- poll command_status with job_id for the final state/signals, do not re-run'}. The NORMAL success path always sets degraded:false so the field is always present (one shared builder to avoid drift). The start-arm error at tools.rs:643 STILL returns Err(into_mcp_error) -- no job_id exists yet, nothing to preserve. degraded/recover_hint/cursor are additive JSON fields; VERIFIED no schema test gates run_and_watch result fields (R3), so no gate breaks, but run_and_watch.v1.json gains invariants documenting degraded/recover_hint.
fork_decisions.rationale: research-gaps explicitly flagged that nobody specified the suture for the discarded job_id. The strict-superset framing (grafted from trust-contract-first, the cleanest articulation) means the agent's existing 'if !complete poll command_status' branch already handles it; degraded:true + recover_hint make the cause explicit and stop the trust-destroying discard of a live job_id. Surfacing cursor (already internal) lets the caller resume rather than restart. Minimal-risk: no new result type, no new IPC variant, no protocol change. Co-implemented with F3 (same loop body).
fork_decisions.decision_short: F2=degraded result as a STRICT SUPERSET of the existing payload + {degraded,recover_hint,cursor}; start-arm failure still Err

fork_decisions.fork: F3 (TC-6 co-design with TC-1b) -- combined status+drain RPC vs slice-arithmetic fix vs cap/doc change
fork_decisions.decision: ADOPT the wall-clock-deadline arithmetic fix (smallest correct); REJECT a new combined status+drain IPC RPC and REJECT a doc-only cap change. Rewrite the wait loop ONCE (shared body tools.rs:650-709): replace `for _ in 0..deadline_slices` (deadline_slices at :650) with `let deadline = Instant::now() + Duration::from_millis(wait_ms)`; loop `while Instant::now() < deadline`; per-iteration BucketWait timeout_ms = min(MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS, remaining_budget_ms) (was hardcoded MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS at tools.rs:683); break when remaining hits zero; PRESERVE the terminal short-circuit (a fast command still returns immediately with all signals). DO NOT change MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS (keep 1_000): the judge graft warns that lowering it to 500 doubles RPC/sec and risks regressing the TC47 load gate (load_noise_backpressure); the wall-clock deadline alone makes the advertised cap honest (total wall <= wait_ms + at most one in-flight slice + RTTs, well under cap) WITHOUT touching slice size, so the load gate is untouched. tokio::time is already in-tree (zero new dep). Co-designed with F2: ONE rewritten loop body, both post-job_id error sites become the degraded-return path, the deadline check replaces the slice counter.
fork_decisions.rationale: research-gaps named TC-6+TC-1b as 'must co-design, same loop body'. A combined RPC is a NEW IPC method (protocol enums + method_name + DISCOVERABLE_METHODS + dispatch + parity test) -- disproportionate machinery that COLLIDES with TC-3's IPC-surface work (unanimous judge fatal flaw on the root-cause-maximalist draft). A doc/cap change is dishonest engineering. Wall-clock-deadline is a localized arithmetic fix that makes the advertised cap true with no protocol/surface change. Keeping the slice at 1000ms (rather than the trust-contract draft's 500ms) sidesteps the load-gate risk the judges caught -- this is the explicit graft: prefer the deadline-only approach unless the load gate is re-run.
fork_decisions.decision_short: F3=wall-clock Instant deadline in the same rewritten loop body as F2; slice stays 1000ms (no load-gate risk); no new RPC; no cap change

fork_decisions.fork: F4 (TC-3 naming/semantics) -- new command_stop vs SPEC-promised command_send_signal; forced-kill-only
fork_decisions.decision: Implement a NEW tool/IPC method named command_stop / IpcRequest::CommandStop (NOT command_send_signal). Forced-kill-only semantics mirroring PtyRuntime::stop (pty_command.rs:526-571): VERIFIED ProcessProbeConfig.grace (process.rs:47) is 'Currently advisory; cancellation in MVP is forced kill only', so the grace window stays unused (deferred, tracked). Daemon kill mechanism: VERIFIED ProcessProbe has cancel_tx: Option<oneshot::Sender<()>> (process.rs:159, set :268) and pub fn cancel() (process.rs:296) does cancel_tx.take() (forced kill). The probe is MOVED into drive_to_exit (command.rs ~:606) inside the spawned waiter, so add ProcessProbe::take_cancel_handle() -> Option<oneshot::Sender<()>> that takes cancel_tx, call it BEFORE the move, and store the sender in a new JobBinding field `cancel: Option<oneshot::Sender<()>>` (JobBinding at command.rs:218-230 currently retains NO cancel handle -- confirmed). Add CommandRuntime::stop(job_id): evaluate PolicyAction::CommandSignal (policy.rs:62, dormant -- VERIFIED it appears only in deny-matrix match arms at 228-230 grouped with CommandStart in the read_only_observer deny set, target_path None at 429, and tests; NO production evaluate() caller), emit the audit row BEFORE the kill (POLICY.md conformance, improving on PTY's ungated stop), take the cancel handle, fire it (forced kill), then jobs.cancel(job_id) synchronously; coordinate with the lifecycle waiter via the existing terminal-state idempotency (PTY checked-then-skipped pattern). Classify CommandStop as NON-idempotent in is_idempotent() (Phase 1 helper) -- it is mutating; a stop on an already-stopped job is harmless but we do not auto-retry mutating RPCs.
fork_decisions.rationale: command_stop is the honest name for forced-kill-only; command_send_signal implies SIGTERM/SIGINT breadth the forced-kill probe cannot honor. Reusing the dormant PolicyAction::CommandSignal (not inventing a variant) honors the global dormant-seam rule AND the tool-control-surface doctrine (a tool needing a policy action must use/add the closed-set variant) without a new variant; policy-gating it (unlike PTY's ungated stop) is the conformant improvement. Cloning/taking the oneshot cancel_tx before the move is strictly smaller and safer than Arc<Mutex<Option<ProcessProbe>>> and avoids the PTY lock-across-await footgun (R2). This is the largest single item (new daemon kill capability + new IPC method + full MCP surface chain), isolated in its own phase.
fork_decisions.decision_short: F4=command_stop (forced-kill), policy-gated via dormant PolicyAction::CommandSignal, take_cancel_handle()+cancel field in JobBinding before the drive_to_exit move, grace deferred

fork_decisions.decision: Host the real spawn round-trip in the IPC handle_self_check (server.rs:801-818, the surface whose false-green misleads LIVE clients), NOT bootstrap run_self_check. Make handle_self_check ASYNC -- and propagate .await to BOTH the primary dispatch arm (server.rs:540-543) AND the alternate dispatch_envelope / Windows named-pipe dispatch path (judge graft: handle_self_check going async forces the alternate transport dispatch to .await too, not just the primary arm). Bucket litter: REUSE ONE CACHED immortal self-check bucket (Mutex<Option<BucketId>> or OnceCell on DaemonState, lazily created on first spawn-running self_check and reused every call), NOT a drop_bucket seam (honors the immortal-bucket invariant source.rs:12-17; BucketSourceTable has no remove). Profile-gate the spawn: skip-or-assert-deny -- if policy denies CommandStart for the active profile (read_only_observer) OR a restrictive command_allow_roots excludes the daemon binary OR repo_only has no resolvable root, SKIP the spawn, keep the existing static report with a 'spawn probe skipped: <profile reason>' line and failures unchanged (NEVER false-RED). Binary: spawn std::env::current_exe() in a hidden no-op/--selfcheck-noop mode (always present, cross-platform, single binary) routed through the normal CommandRuntime path so it inherits policy + emits a command_start audit row; poll command_status to terminal within a tight bounded budget (~2s); set failures>0 ONLY on genuine breakage (spawn error, never reaches terminal, nonzero exit).
fork_decisions.rationale: R4 recommended the IPC handler (higher value, the surface a live client polls) but didn't commit; minimal-risk commits to it because that is the surface that lied during the TC-1/TC-6 window. Cached bucket honors the immortal-bucket invariant and accumulates exactly ONE bucket over the daemon lifetime, vs a drop_bucket seam that touches BucketManager + BucketSourceTable (no remove) -- much larger blast radius, deferred. Profile-gating to skip-not-fail is mandatory to avoid flipping healthy read_only_observer daemons RED. current_exe() avoids baking in a platform binary. The dispatch_envelope await propagation (grafted from trust-contract-first/root-cause-maximalist) prevents a compile/await break on the Windows named-pipe path the minimal-risk draft missed. A NEGATIVE test (forced round-trip failure => failures>0) proves it is no longer a hardcoded green.
fork_decisions.decision_short: F5=spawn probe in async handle_self_check, await propagated to primary AND dispatch_envelope arms, ONE cached immortal bucket, profile-gated skip-or-assert-deny, current_exe() no-op, negative test

fork_decisions.fork: F6 (TC-4 argv shape + CLI render scope)
fork_decisions.decision: Surface a BOUNDED argv head (argv[0..2] = program + first arg) via the existing format_argv_metadata-style redaction (command.rs:989-1004), NOT full argv. Add two additive fields to ProbeListEntry (protocol.rs, struct used by collect_probes at runtime.rs:13-86; both #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if="Option::is_none")] matching the frames_suppressed/liveness additive precedent): tag: Option<String> and argv_head: Option<Vec<String>>. In collect_probes: Command arm (currently hardcodes path:None at ~:37) reads argv via a new bounded accessor on CommandRuntime (per-job argv_head from the JobManager record's config.argv, redacted) and lifts tag (+ path) from state.sources.get(bucket_id) (BucketSource); PTY arm (currently discards _argv at ~:67, path:None at ~:80) stops discarding _argv and lifts tag from sources; FileWatch arm synthesizes argv_head=['file_watch:<path>']. ALSO fix the run_and_watch tag plumbing (VERIFIED fake-success path): into_parts() hardcodes tag:None (tools.rs:2277) -- add #[serde(default)] pub tag: Option<String> to McpRunAndWatchParams (struct at tools.rs:2219) and set tag: self.tag in into_parts(). CLI render.rs IS IN SCOPE (additively): display the new tag/argv_head columns; Option fields degrade to blank so an omitted column is no regression, but render.rs is updated so fields are not silently dropped (R4 risk).
fork_decisions.rationale: RISK_REGISTER R-06 establishes argv is already surfaced verbatim in command_exited summary and exempted from the TC47 leak check, so over-redaction would break parity; the reconciliation is to bound to the head (program + first arg, enough to identify the job) AND route through the existing redaction helper -- more conservative than the existing verbatim summary, consistent with R-06. serde(default) additive fields are the established non-breaking pattern (R4 confirmed, uncontested; old client ignores unknown, old daemon omits, new client sees None). Fixing into_parts() is mandatory -- R3 flagged it as a latent fake-success path (compiles + passes all tests but silently drops tag). render.rs in-scope prevents the silent-drop risk.
fork_decisions.decision_short: F6=argv_head (argv[0..2], redacted) + tag as serde(default) ProbeListEntry fields; fix into_parts tag:None; render.rs additively in scope

fork_decisions.fork: F7 (sequencing into PR-sized phases, each independently CI-green)
fork_decisions.decision: Seven phases, each a separate Conventional-Commit PR to a review branch, each <=10 files, each independently CI-green on BOTH OS gates. ORDER (by production-harm reduction): Phase 0 (planning artifacts + ledger rows, docs-only, NO code) -> Phase 1 (F1=a retry-gate + honest remedy text, PURE LOGIC, the urgent double-spawn regression kill) -> Phase 2 (F1 narrow nonce-keyed in-flight dedup, the stateful TC-2 closure, isolated from the urgent Phase 1) -> Phase 3 (F2+F3 co-designed wait-loop rewrite, TC-1b+TC-6, ONE body) -> Phase 4 (F6 TC-4 probe-row identity + run_and_watch tag + CLI render) -> Phase 5 (F5 TC-5 self_check real spawn round-trip) -> Phase 6 (F4 TC-3 command_stop, the only multi-surface change, isolated LAST). TC-3 last because it is the only phase that perturbs the tool-count surface (37->38) and the 5 name-lists/3 count-assertions/fixture-map/system_discover fixture/minimal_tool_args -- isolating it means the other six phases never touch that fragile atomic set, keeping each independently green.
fork_decisions.rationale: Minimal-risk + independently-shippable: Phase 1 alone stops the production double-spawn and merges immediately (pure logic, smallest blast radius). Phase 2 adds the stateful dedup that genuinely closes TC-2, separated per the unanimous judge note that folding it into Phase 1 is a scope miss. F2+F3 MUST share one phase (same loop body; research-gaps: two separate edits collide). TC-3 is last because it carries all the atomic-count churn and the release-please '29-tool' doc reconciliation. Each new tool gets a daemon-IPC integration test; each phase names its exact verification commands.
fork_decisions.decision_short: F7=7 phases (0:docs, 1:F1-retry-gate pure-logic, 2:dedup, 3:F2+F3 wait-loop, 4:TC-4+CLI, 5:TC-5, 6:TC-3 command_stop) ordered by harm reduction; TC-3 isolated last

phases.name: Phase 0 -- Planning artifacts + ledger rows (NO code)
defects: meta: establishes tracking for TC-1..TC-6 per HARD CONSTRAINTS (BACKLOG/RISK_REGISTER/.planning open-work shape) before any corrective code lands
changes: .planning/tc-bugfix-campaign/00-INDEX.md -- new campaign index in the open-work multi-file shape: header (Repo/branch/commit), executive-summary table (TC-id | Priority | Status | Blocks-product-promise), mermaid dependency graph, recommended phase order, planning-artifacts table, ledger-corrections table superseding stale tool-count claims (README/RELEASE_CHECKLIST '29', TOOL_CONTROL_SURFACE '32', live assertions '37') with file:line

.planning/tc-bugfix-campaign/PLAN-TC1-ghost-spawn.md, PLAN-TC2-dedup.md, PLAN-TC1b-TC6-waitloop.md, PLAN-TC4-probe-identity.md, PLAN-TC5-selfcheck-spawn.md, PLAN-TC3-command-stop.md -- per-defect PLAN.md each: Source / Posture / Summary-table (Symptom | Location-lines | Fix-sketch | Effort | Test-impact) then per-item Symptom / Citations (fenced start:end:path) / Fix / Effort / Test, mirroring the existing .planning/open-work PLAN.md format

BACKLOG.md -- replace the empty P0 'None' with P0 rows (TC-1a double-spawn regression, TC-1b lost-job-handle) and P1 rows (TC-2 dedup guard, TC-3 command_stop, TC-4 anonymous probe rows, TC-5 self_check false-green, TC-6 wait-cap overrun); plus DEFERRED rows: server-honored idempotency key (F1-b), pre-spawn ack (F1-c), command_stop graceful grace window (F4), numeric -32603 wire-code change

RISK_REGISTER.md -- ADD new Active rows (never edit/remove existing per the immutability rule): R-07 ghost-job / client-timeout-aborts-live-spawn / blind-retry-of-mutating-RPC (mitigation: Phase 1 + Phase 2), R-08 self_check false-green (mitigation: Phase 5), R-09 run_and_watch advertised wait-cap not honored (mitigation: Phase 3); cross-link R-06 for the argv-surface precedent. Rows stay Active until each fix lands WITH proof
tests: none (docs only); source-status N/A
verification: wsl bash scripts/linux-gate.sh  (docs-only PRs are NOT path-filtered: npm-binary-build.yml has no paths filter, so the full required gate still runs -- expected, not a failure)
pwsh -File scripts/windows-gate.ps1
git diff --stat shows ONLY .planning/, BACKLOG.md, RISK_REGISTER.md (no code files)
manual: mermaid graph parses; every TC row cites file:line; no existing RISK_REGISTER row touched
risks: docs-only PR still triggers the full 8-required-context CI (no paths filter, by design) -- expected
MUST NOT touch existing RISK_REGISTER rows (append-only immutability rule)

phases.name: Phase 1 -- F1=(a): stop blind-retrying mutating RPCs + honest remedy text (URGENT regression kill, PURE LOGIC)
defects: TC-1a: daemon_client.rs:191-198 blindly re-sends a cloned CommandStartCombed on any transport error => double-spawn (regression at HEAD)

sub: transport_unavailable_error (tools.rs:1798) routes through McpError::internal_error (numeric -32603, doc comment falsely claims otherwise -- VERIFIED) and its remedy literally says 'retry the tool' -- fatal for a mutating op
TC-2 (partial: the AUTOMATIC auto-retry double-spawn path is eliminated here; the stateful dedup guard for the manual-retry path lands in Phase 2)
changes: crates/ipc/src/protocol.rs (IpcRequest enum at :195) -- add `pub const fn is_idempotent(&self) -> bool` (additive): EXHAUSTIVE match returning false for CommandStartCombed, PtyCommandStart, PtyCommandWriteStdin, RegistryUpsert, RegistryActivate, RegistryDeactivate, RegistryImportPack, FileWatchStart, FileWatchStop, Shutdown (and any other state-mutating variant); true for reads (Health, CommandStatus, BucketWait, RuntimeState, ProbeList, ProbeStatus, BucketEventsSince, BucketSummary, EventContext, RegistrySearch/Get/Test/ListActive, FileReadWindow/Search/WatchList, PtyCommandList, SubscriptionPull/List/Seek, SystemDiscover, PolicyStatus, SelfCheck). Exhaustive so a future variant forces a deliberate classification.

crates/mcp/src/daemon_client.rs:191 -- change the retry guard from `Err(e) if e.is_transport()` to `Err(e) if e.is_transport() && request.is_idempotent()`; a transport failure on a NON-idempotent request returns Err(e) immediately (no try_self_heal re-send). Update the doc comment (:155-186) to state mutating RPCs are NOT auto-retried. Stays spawn/fs/socket-free (pure bool read) -- guard-safe.

crates/mcp/src/tools.rs:1798 -- split transport_unavailable_error into mutating/non-mutating variants OR add a bool param; the mutating message DROPS 'retry the tool' and says 'the command MAY have started; call command_status or runtime_state to confirm whether it took effect before re-running' (still code:daemon_unavailable; numeric -32603 unchanged this phase, tracked in R-07 as a separate decision)

crates/mcp/src/tools.rs:1667 (into_mcp_error) -- it short-circuits transport BEFORE the code match and has NO IpcRequest in scope (VERIFIED); add a sibling `into_mcp_error_mutating(&IpcError)` (or thread a bool) used by the mutating tool arms (run_and_watch start at tools.rs:643 and command_start_combed) so the mutating remedy is produced; read-only arms keep into_mcp_error
tests: unit (crates/ipc, #[cfg(test)]): is_idempotent() returns false for every mutating variant and true for every read variant (table-driven, exhaustive enough that an unclassified future variant is obvious) -- source-status: test-only

unit (crates/mcp daemon_client tests, mock inner.call): a mutating request hitting a transport error is NOT retried (inner.call invoked == 1); a read request IS retried (== 2) -- source-status: test-only/mock
unit (crates/mcp): the mutating remedy text contains no 'retry the tool' and instructs command_status/runtime_state reconciliation -- source-status: test-only

integration through daemon IPC (TESTING.md 3.4, own TEST socket + data dir): start a real >5s-spawning command via run_and_watch and assert exactly ONE job appears in runtime_state (no duplicate) -- source-status: live
verification: wsl bash scripts/linux-gate.sh  (fmt --check + clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings + nextest --workspace + TC47 load gate + MCP guard 1 (no Command/spawn/TcpListener/UdpSocket in crates/mcp/src) + guard 2 (no fs) -- daemon_client edit is pure data, guard-safe + rustc==1.95.0)
pwsh -File scripts/windows-gate.ps1  (windows_no_console_spawn + windows_spawn_site_coverage; touches the IPC transport path used by the cfg(windows) named-pipe client)
cargo nextest run -p terminal-commander-ipc -p terminal-commander-mcp
manual: drive a >5s-spawning command via run_and_watch on a TEST socket; confirm ONE job, not two; confirm the mutating-failure envelope no longer says 'retry the tool'
risks: is_idempotent MUST be exhaustive so a future variant is classified deliberately (compile-forced)

FileWatchStart/RegistryActivate are arguably idempotent-ish but are state-creating -- classify as non-idempotent (conservative; worst case is a non-retry the agent can re-issue, never a silent double-effect)

into_mcp_error has no request in scope (VERIFIED) -- the mutating remedy MUST be threaded from the call site or the honest text never appears (the under-specified trap the judges flagged on terser drafts)
crates/mcp/src must stay spawn/fs/socket-free (guards 1&2) -- the edit only reads a bool and forwards an error

phases.name: Phase 2 -- F1 defense-in-depth: narrow nonce-keyed daemon in-flight dedup (genuinely closes TC-2)
defects: TC-2: no idempotency/dedup guard anywhere in the daemon (command.rs start_combed has no pre-spawn lookup; RequestEnvelope has no key) -- VERIFIED. The residual double-spawn path after Phase 1 is a MANUAL caller/LLM re-call of a timed-out mutating start; this phase closes it without a protocol change
changes: crates/daemon/src/command.rs (~:540, before the id mint at :472) -- add a short-TTL in-flight fingerprint map (HashMap<u64,(JobId,BucketId)> behind the existing live lock or a dedicated Mutex on CommandRuntime) checked at the TOP of start_combed: keyed PREFERENTIALLY on a client-supplied request nonce (see protocol field below) and falling back to std-hash(argv + cwd + tag) ONLY within a very short window (~3s); a duplicate within the window returns the SAME (job_id,bucket_id) instead of spawning. Uses std hashing (zero new dep).

crates/daemon/src/command.rs -- evict the fingerprint on EVERY completion path: normal exit (lifecycle waiter ~:687), cancel, AND the spawn-failure early-return (command.rs:548-560). A leaked entry must never block a legitimate re-run -- the TTL is the backstop, eviction-on-completion is the primary.

crates/ipc/src/protocol.rs -- add an OPTIONAL #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if="Option::is_none")] dedup_nonce: Option<String> to CommandStartParams (additive, non-breaking; old client omits => fall back to argv+cwd+tag window keying). This is NOT a server-honored idempotency-key protocol (no TTL store, no envelope change) -- just an in-flight collapse hint.

crates/mcp/src/tools.rs -- when run_and_watch / command_start_combed retries are gated off (Phase 1), the adapter may optionally set dedup_nonce on the start params so a deliberate caller re-issue collapses; default None preserves current behavior
tests: integration (crates/daemon, own socket+data dir): two identical CommandStartCombed within the TTL window (same nonce, or same argv+cwd+tag) return the SAME job_id (deduped, one process) -- source-status: live

integration (RACE/never-collapse, the judge-flagged correctness guard): two legitimately-DISTINCT rapid identical-argv runs with DISTINCT nonces spawn TWO distinct jobs (no false collapse); and a third run AFTER the first completes (fingerprint evicted) spawns a fresh job (never blocked) -- source-status: live
integration (eviction-on-spawn-failure): a start that fails to spawn evicts its fingerprint so an immediate legitimate retry is not blocked -- source-status: live
unit (crates/ipc): CommandStartParams with absent dedup_nonce decodes (serde default); old payload round-trips -- source-status: test-only
verification: wsl bash scripts/linux-gate.sh  (fmt + clippy -D + nextest + TC47 load gate + MCP guards; the dedup is daemon-side, mcp/src untouched except the optional nonce passthrough which adds no spawn/fs/socket)
pwsh -File scripts/windows-gate.ps1  (start_combed spawn path is cfg-split; windows_spawn_site_coverage stays >=1)
cargo nextest run -p terminal-commander-daemon -p terminal-commander-ipc
manual: on a TEST socket, fire two identical starts within 3s and confirm one job in runtime_state; fire two with distinct nonces and confirm two jobs
risks: JUDGE FATAL-FLAW FIX: a naive fingerprint silently collapses two legitimately-distinct rapid identical runs. Mitigated by (1) nonce-preferred keying (distinct nonces never collapse), (2) a very short window for the argv+cwd+tag fallback, (3) the explicit never-collapse + never-block tests above.
the fingerprint MUST be evicted on exit, cancel, AND spawn-failure or it leaks/blocks a legitimate re-run (its own failure mode) -- covered by the eviction-on-every-path change + test
this phase is STATEFUL daemon behavior and is deliberately SEPARATE from the urgent pure-logic Phase 1 (judge scope-discipline note)
dedup_nonce is additive serde(default) -- no wire break, no protocol version bump

phases.name: Phase 3 -- F2+F3: wait-loop rewrite (job_id-preserving degraded result + wall-clock deadline)
defects: TC-1b: wait loop returns Err(into_mcp_error) on any mid-loop RPC failure (tools.rs:665 CommandStatus arm, :703 BucketWait arm), discarding the known job_id/bucket_id/cursor/signals -- VERIFIED

TC-6: wait_ms=60000 cap self-violates -- `for _ in 0..deadline_slices` (tools.rs:650) x per-slice BucketWait blocking up to MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS=1000 (tools.rs:683) + RTTs => ~62-70s wall vs 60000ms advertised -- VERIFIED
changes: crates/mcp/src/tools.rs:650-709 -- rewrite the wait loop ONCE: replace `let deadline_slices = wait_ms.div_ceil(MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS).max(1)` + `for _ in 0..deadline_slices` with `let deadline = Instant::now() + Duration::from_millis(wait_ms)`; loop `while Instant::now() < deadline`; per-iteration BucketWait timeout_ms = Some(if terminal {0} else { min(MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS, remaining_ms) }) (was hardcoded MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS at :683); PRESERVE the terminal short-circuit (tools.rs:705-707) so a fast command returns immediately with all signals. Keep MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS=1000 (no load-gate risk).

crates/mcp/src/tools.rs:665 and :703 -- both post-job_id error arms change from `Err(e) => return Err(into_mcp_error(&e))` to: build & return the DEGRADED partial payload via a shared helper run_and_watch_degraded_result(job_id, bucket_id, cursor, signals, last_state, last_exit_code) returning isError:false with {..., complete:false, wait_exhausted:true, degraded:true, recover_hint}. The start-arm error at tools.rs:643 STILL returns Err (no job_id yet).

crates/mcp/src/tools.rs:724-736 -- the NORMAL success payload always includes degraded:false (one shared builder so the normal and degraded paths cannot drift); add recover_hint:null on the normal path

crates/mcp/src/tools.rs:617 -- update the run_and_watch #[tool] description: the wait_ms cap is a WALL-CLOCK budget honored within one slice + RTT; on a mid-wait interruption it returns a degraded job-identified result (poll command_status), never a bare error once a job_id exists

tests/fixtures/contracts/mcp-tools/run_and_watch.v1.json -- add invariants documenting degraded/recover_hint/cursor (convention per docs/contracts/README.md; VERIFIED no schema test gates run_and_watch result fields, so additive, not a hard gate)
tests: unit (crates/mcp): run_and_watch_degraded_result carries job_id + bucket_id + cursor + degraded:true + recover_hint and is isError:false-shaped; the normal builder sets degraded:false -- source-status: test-only
unit (crates/mcp): wall-deadline arithmetic -- at wait_ms=60000 the computed per-slice timeout never exceeds remaining and the loop cannot exceed deadline + one slice -- source-status: test-only

integration through daemon IPC (3.4, TEST socket): (a) a command that exits within budget returns complete:true, degraded:false with all signals; (b) a non-terminating command with wait_ms=2000 returns wait_exhausted:true + complete:false + job_id present and measured wall time <= ~2.5s; (c) a long-running command with wait_ms=60000 returns measured wall <= 61s (TC-6 acceptance) -- source-status: live

integration: inject a mid-loop IPC error AFTER job_id is known (mock daemon errors on the 2nd CommandStatus) and assert isError:false + degraded:true + the correct preserved job_id (TC-1b recovery) -- source-status: live (partial/mock for the injection)
verification: wsl bash scripts/linux-gate.sh  (fmt + clippy -D + nextest + TC47 load gate (UNCHANGED since slice stays 1000ms) + MCP guards; pure crates/mcp/src edit, Instant via tokio::time already in-tree, no fs/socket)
pwsh -File scripts/windows-gate.ps1
cargo nextest run -p terminal-commander-mcp  (incl. the e2e bucket flow in crates/mcp/tests must still pass)
timing assertion in the long-running integration test proves wall <= advertised cap + bounded margin (TC-6 acceptance); assert the mid-loop-error case never returns isError:true once job_id is known
risks: F2 and F3 MUST be one phase (shared loop body tools.rs:650-709; separate edits collide -- research-gaps explicit warning)
the loop rewrite must NOT drop the final signals on a fast exit -- preserve the terminal short-circuit and drain semantics
degraded/recover_hint/cursor are additive JSON; no run_and_watch result-schema test exists (R3) so no gate breaks, but the e2e flow must still pass
keeping MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS at 1000ms is deliberate -- it avoids the load-gate (load_noise_backpressure) RPC-doubling risk the judges flagged on the 500ms variant

phases.name: Phase 4 -- F6: TC-4 runtime_state probe-row identity (argv_head + tag) + run_and_watch tag + CLI render
defects: TC-4: collect_probes (runtime.rs:13-86) hardcodes path:None on the Command arm (~:37) and PTY arm (~:80), discards PTY _argv (~:67); ProbeListEntry has no tag/argv field; run_and_watch into_parts hardcodes tag:None (tools.rs:2277, VERIFIED) and McpRunAndWatchParams lacks a tag field
changes: crates/ipc/src/protocol.rs (ProbeListEntry struct) -- add #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if="Option::is_none")] pub tag: Option<String> and pub argv_head: Option<Vec<String>> (additive, matching the frames_suppressed/liveness serde precedent; non-breaking, no version bump). Doc the bounded redacted argv[0..2] shape.

crates/daemon/src/command.rs -- add a bounded argv accessor (e.g. per-job argv_head(job_id) -> Option<Vec<String>> returning argv[0..2] projected through the existing format_argv_metadata-style redaction at command.rs:989-1004) so collect_probes need not re-implement redaction or hold extra locks

crates/daemon/src/ipc/handlers/runtime.rs:13-86 -- in collect_probes: Command arm reads argv_head via the new accessor and lifts tag (+ path) from state.sources.get(j.bucket_id) (BucketSource); PTY arm stops discarding _argv and projects argv_head + lifts tag from sources; FileWatch arm synthesizes argv_head=['file_watch:<path>'] and keeps its existing path

crates/mcp/src/tools.rs:2219 (McpRunAndWatchParams) -- add #[serde(default)] pub tag: Option<String> (mirror McpCommandStartParams.tag); tools.rs:2277 -- set tag: self.tag in into_parts() (replace the hardcoded tag:None fake-success path)

crates/cli/src/render.rs -- render the new tag/argv_head columns in the runtime_state/probe_list view (downstream consumer of RuntimeStateResponse; Option fields degrade to blank so an omitted column is no regression, but render.rs is updated so fields are not silently dropped)

tests/fixtures/contracts/mcp-tools/runtime_state.v1.json (+ probe_list.v1.json if it carries sample rows) -- update example rows to show tag/argv_head (convention; VERIFIED the fixture_catalogue_contract does not byte-gate individual row fields)
tests: unit (crates/ipc): ProbeListEntry with absent tag/argv_head decodes (serde default); old payload round-trips -- source-status: test-only
unit (crates/mcp): McpRunAndWatchParams::into_parts threads tag (regression for the silent-drop) -- source-status: test-only

integration through daemon IPC (3.4, TEST socket): start a tagged command, call runtime_state, assert the probe row carries the tag and a bounded redacted argv_head (argv[0..2]) and path where applicable -- source-status: live
integration: run_and_watch with tag=X -> runtime_state row shows tag X (proves end-to-end into_parts plumbing) -- source-status: live
unit (crates/daemon): an argv with a secret-looking token surfaces redacted in argv_head consistent with format_argv_metadata (parity with R-06 posture) -- source-status: test-only
CLI render assertion that tag/argv_head columns appear -- source-status: live

manual: runtime_state rows are no longer anonymous (tag + argv_head + path present); confirm no lock-ordering inversion (sources.get(bucket_id) read lock vs the live/jobs locks already held in collect_probes -- up to MAX_LIST_LIMIT=500 reads per call)
risks: argv redaction MUST reuse format_argv_metadata and bound to [0..2] -- over-redaction breaks parity with the existing verbatim command_exited summary (R-06); under-redaction leaks secrets. Pick the bounded-head + existing-redaction posture.
up to 500 sources.get() RwLock reads per runtime_state -- verify no lock inversion with the live/jobs locks held in collect_probes (R4 risk); likely fine (read locks, no held-across-await)
render.rs in scope so new fields are not silently dropped (R4)
ProbeListEntry is constructed only in collect_probes -- the additive fields fix all three consumers (runtime_state/probe_list/probe_status) without touching other struct literals

phases.name: Phase 5 -- F5: TC-5 self_check real command-spawn round-trip (async handler, cached bucket, profile-gated)
defects: TC-5: handle_self_check (server.rs:801-818) hardcodes failures:0 and never spawns; the dispatch arm (server.rs:540-543) is sync; buckets are immortal (source.rs:12-17, no drop_bucket) -- VERIFIED
changes: crates/daemon/src/ipc/server.rs:801-818 -- make handle_self_check `async fn`; keep the static lines (data_dir / policy_profile / audit / audit_count) and ADD a profile-gated bounded real round-trip: if policy allows CommandStart for the active profile AND command_allow_roots does not exclude the daemon binary AND (repo_only has a resolvable root or profile is not repo_only) -> spawn std::env::current_exe() in a hidden --selfcheck-noop mode via the normal CommandRuntime path into the CACHED self-check bucket, poll command_status to terminal within a ~2s budget, set failures>0 + an explanatory report line ONLY on genuine breakage (spawn error / never terminal / nonzero exit); ELSE SKIP and emit 'spawn probe skipped: <profile reason>' with failures unchanged (NEVER false-RED)
crates/daemon/src/ipc/server.rs:540-543 -- primary dispatch arm gains .await for the now-async handle_self_check (mirror the bucket/pty async arms)

crates/daemon/src/ipc/server.rs (dispatch_envelope / alternate Windows named-pipe dispatch path) -- ALSO gains .await for handle_self_check (judge graft: the async change forces BOTH dispatch sites, not just the primary arm)

crates/daemon/src/state.rs (DaemonState) -- add a cached self-check bucket holder (Mutex<Option<BucketId>> or OnceCell) lazily set on the first spawn-running self_check and reused thereafter (honors the immortal-bucket invariant source.rs:12-17; no drop_bucket)
crates/daemon/src/main.rs (or the bin entry) -- add the hidden --selfcheck-noop mode to current_exe() that exits 0 immediately (the portable always-present cross-platform probe target)
crates/ipc/src/protocol.rs (SelfCheckResponse) -- structurally unchanged {report, failures}; the report string gains a spawn-probe line (no wire change)
tests: integration through daemon IPC (3.4, TEST socket, DeveloperLocal profile): self_check spawns the noop round-trip, report shows the spawn-ok line, failures==0 on a healthy daemon; a SECOND self_check reuses the SAME bucket (bucket_count grows by at most 1 over the daemon lifetime) -- source-status: live
integration: under read_only_observer profile, self_check SKIPS the spawn and stays failures==0 (NOT RED) with 'spawn probe skipped: <reason>' -- source-status: live

negative (TC-5 acceptance, the judge-graft proof it is no longer a hardcoded green): force the round-trip to fail (e.g. a test build where the noop exits nonzero, or an impossible argv on a dev profile) and assert failures>0 with an explanatory line -- source-status: live
verification: wsl bash scripts/linux-gate.sh  (spawn path is cfg(unix)/cfg(windows); current_exe + CREATE_NO_WINDOW on Windows; windows_spawn_site_coverage stays >=1)

risks: MUST NOT restart/kill the live machine daemon -- all tests use their own TC_SOCKET + explicit data dir (AGENTS.md)
profile-gating MUST skip-not-fail under read_only_observer / restrictive command_allow_roots / repo_only-no-root (avoid false-RED)
current_exe() in --selfcheck-noop mode must be SKIPPED (not failed) if command_allow_roots excludes the daemon binary

async signature change to handle_self_check forces BOTH the primary dispatch arm AND the dispatch_envelope/named-pipe path to .await -- verify the Windows path too (judge graft; the minimal-risk draft missed the alternate dispatch site)
self_check now emits a command_start audit row per spawn-running call -- confirm it does not trip idle self-reap (self_check already bumps activity; the spawn is bounded)

phases.name: Phase 6 -- F4: TC-3 command_stop (daemon kill capability + new IPC method + full MCP surface, ATOMIC count churn)
defects: TC-3: no command-job stop. CommandRuntime has no stop/cancel/kill; JobBinding (command.rs:218-230) retains no cancel handle -- VERIFIED; no CommandStop IPC method; no MCP tool; PolicyAction::CommandSignal dormant (policy.rs:62) -- VERIFIED
changes: crates/probes/src/process.rs -- add `pub fn take_cancel_handle(&mut self) -> Option<oneshot::Sender<()>>` that takes cancel_tx (process.rs:159, set :268) so the handle can be retained before the probe moves; the existing pub fn cancel() at :296 stays for the in-probe forced kill

crates/daemon/src/command.rs:218-230 -- add `cancel: Option<oneshot::Sender<()>>` to JobBinding; at the spawn site (~command.rs:548, BEFORE the `drive_to_exit(probe)` move at ~:606) call probe.take_cancel_handle() and store the sender in the JobBinding constructed at ~:589. Add `pub fn stop(&self, job_id) -> Result<(BucketId, ProcessProbeMetrics), CommandError>`: evaluate PolicyAction::CommandSignal (policy.rs:62, dormant, correct deny matrix at policy.rs:228-230), emit the audit row BEFORE the kill (POLICY.md conformance), remove the live binding, fire the cancel handle (forced kill, grace unused), then jobs.cancel(job_id) synchronously; coordinate with the lifecycle waiter via the existing terminal-state idempotency (PTY checked-then-skipped pattern)

crates/ipc/src/protocol.rs -- add CommandStopParams{job_id} + CommandStopResponse{job_id,bucket_id,counters} (mirror PtyCommandStop), IpcRequest::CommandStop + IpcResponse::CommandStop; classify CommandStop as NON-idempotent in is_idempotent() (Phase 1 helper); re-export the new types through crates/ipc/src/lib.rs

crates/daemon/src/ipc/server.rs -- method_name() exhaustive arm (+CommandStop="command_stop"), DISCOVERABLE_METHODS const (+"command_stop"), dispatch() arm (handle_command_stop), all_request_variants() test helper line, and the system_discover_methods_match_dispatch parity test cross-check -- ALL atomic or the parity test fails both directions; re-exports through crates/daemon/src/ipc/mod.rs + lib.rs

crates/mcp/src/tools.rs -- new #[tool] async fn command_stop forwarding IpcRequest::CommandStop (ensure_daemon_available first; NEVER spawns/kills from the mcp crate -- daemon owns the kill, guard 1 safe); McpCommandStopParams{job_id}; ADD command_stop to tool_catalogue() (status:Live)

crates/mcp/src/tools.rs -- ATOMIC count/list updates: catalogue_lists_thirty_seven_live_tools -> _thirty_eight_ and add command_stop in the ORDERED vec (after run_and_watch/command_status, mirroring the family grouping); tool_router_exposes_all_live_tools add command_stop in the SORTED vec at its alphabetical slot (after "command_status", before "event_context" -- VERIFIED sort order: command_output_tail < command_start_combed < command_status < command_stop); update module/lib doc tool counts

crates/mcp/tests/mcp_stdio.rs + mcp_live_daemon.rs -- insert command_stop in the SORTED name lists (after command_status) and bump the count string 37->38; daemon_unavailable_envelope.rs:215 -- bump assert_eq!(checked, 36 -> 37) and its comment ('expected 37 daemon-backed tools (38 catalogue entries minus system_discover)'); add a minimal_tool_args arm for command_stop (job_id-only; may reuse the existing command_status|pty_command_stop arm)

tests/fixtures/contracts/mcp-tool-fixture-map.v1.json -- add command_stop to live_tools[] and daemon_unavailable_shapes; bump counts live_tools 37->38 and covered_live 33->34 (VERIFIED current values); add tests/fixtures/contracts/mcp-tools/command_stop.v1.json (copy the pty_command_stop.v1.json shape); add command_stop to tests/fixtures/contracts/mcp-tools/system_discover.v1.json tools[]

Windows + unix: kill path under both cfg gates -- source-status: live
verification: wsl bash scripts/linux-gate.sh  (MCP guard 1: command_stop forwards to the daemon, NO Command/spawn/TcpListener/UdpSocket in crates/mcp/src; guard 2: no fs; the system_discover parity test; nextest --workspace must be green with ALL 5 name lists + 3 count assertions consistent at 38)

risks: LARGEST phase: 5 hardcoded name lists (tools.rs ordered, tools.rs sorted, mcp_stdio.rs, mcp_live_daemon.rs, system_discover.v1.json) + 3 count assertions (daemon_unavailable_envelope checked 36->37, catalogue test name thirty_seven->thirty_eight, mcp_live_daemon count string) + fixture map (live_tools 37->38, covered_live 33->34) + system_discover fixture + minimal_tool_args MUST update ATOMICALLY in one commit or CI red (the single biggest half-fix trap; all values VERIFIED)

the new IPC method updates 6+ sync surfaces atomically: IpcRequest/IpcResponse + Params/Response structs, method_name() exhaustive match, DISCOVERABLE_METHODS, dispatch() arm, all_request_variants helper, re-exports (ipc/lib.rs, daemon ipc/mod.rs + lib.rs) -- or the parity test fails both directions

take the cancel handle BEFORE the probe moves into drive_to_exit (command.rs ~:606) -- do NOT store the whole probe (avoids the PTY Arc<Mutex> lock-across-await footgun); the oneshot send is non-blocking
stop() vs the lifecycle waiter race on jobs.cancel -- rely on the existing terminal-state idempotency (PTY precedent) and add the race test
MCP guard 1: the kill MUST be daemon-side; any accidental Command/spawn in the mcp command_stop handler fails CI

release side-effect: a feat:/fix: commit triggers release-please; the tool-surface change breaks the stale '29-tool unchanged' release-doc invariant -- reconcile here and coordinate the merge with the operator (NO push/merge without approval)
grace window deferred (forced-kill-only, process.rs:47) -- tracked in BACKLOG (Phase 0)

explicitly_deferred: F1-(b) server-honored idempotency key on RequestEnvelope: DEFERRED to BACKLOG + RISK_REGISTER R-07. With the blind retry of mutating RPCs removed (Phase 1) and the narrow daemon in-flight dedup landed (Phase 2), both the AUTOMATIC and the realistic MANUAL double-spawn windows are closed without a wire/protocol change. The full idempotency-key protocol has unresolved design (client-id vs argv/cwd/window fingerprint, TTL/dedup-window, persistence) the researchers left open; the in-flight dedup covers the realistic duplicate window at far lower blast radius. Tracked, not dropped.

F1-(c) pre-spawn async ack handshake: DEFERRED to BACKLOG. It rewrites the spawn-failure contract (command.rs:548-560 must flip an already-acked Starting job to Failed and synthesize a command_failed via bucket/audit) and exposes a new Starting liveness at the wire boundary that run_and_watch/command_status/liveness must learn; research-gaps confirms it STILL double-spawns under blind retry unless paired with a dedup guard -- so once Phase 1 (no-retry) + Phase 2 (dedup) exist it is redundant, highest-blast-radius machinery. Tracked with R-07.

F3 combined status+drain wait RPC for TC-6: DEFERRED (not adopted). The wall-clock-deadline arithmetic fix (Phase 3) makes the advertised cap honest with zero protocol surface; a combined RPC is a NEW IPC method (full method_name/DISCOVERABLE_METHODS/dispatch/parity chain) that COLLIDES with TC-3's IPC work. Recorded as a possible future latency optimization in the .planning index, not a tracked obligation.

Changing the numeric JSON-RPC code of transport_unavailable_error away from -32603 (tools.rs:1798, VERIFIED it currently routes through McpError::internal_error): DEFERRED to RISK_REGISTER R-07. Phase 1 fixes the BEHAVIOR (no retry of mutating ops) and the misleading 'retry the tool' remedy text; whether to also change the wire numeric code for clients keying off -32603 is a separate decision with client-compat implications, flagged but not forced this campaign.

F4 command_stop graceful-stop grace window: DEFERRED to BACKLOG. ProcessProbeConfig.grace exists but is VERIFIED 'advisory; cancellation in MVP is forced kill only' (process.rs:47); wiring a graceful SIGTERM-then-SIGKILL window is net-new probe work, not tool exposure. command_stop ships forced-kill-only (parity with PtyRuntime::stop), which satisfies TC-3's stop requirement. Tracked.

Retrofitting policy-gating onto the existing ungated pty_command_stop (PtyRuntime::stop, pty_command.rs:526-571): DEFERRED -- out of TC-1..TC-6 scope. command_stop is gated correctly via the dormant CommandSignal; PTY symmetry is a separate conformance item noted in the .planning index.

A real drop_bucket seam (BucketManager + BucketSourceTable.remove): DEFERRED -- TC-5 reuses ONE cached immortal self-check bucket instead, honoring the existing immortal-bucket invariant (source.rs:12-17). A reclamation seam is larger blast radius and not required.

Broad reconciliation of the pervasive stale doc tool-counts (29 in README/RELEASE_CHECKLIST, 32 in TOOL_CONTROL_SURFACE) beyond the lines command_stop must touch: only the CI-gated assertions + the lines this campaign changes are updated. A full doc-count sweep is a separate chore noted in the .planning index.
Reconciling CONTRIBUTING dead branch/goal-file doctrine and any SPEC internal Tier-1 drift: flagged in the .planning index as doc-vs-reality drift to surface, not corrected by this defect campaign.
invariants: ZERO new dependencies: all fixes use in-tree primitives -- tokio::time (Instant/Duration) for the wall-clock deadline, std hashing for the in-flight dedup fingerprint, the existing oneshot::Sender for the cancel handle, serde(default) for additive fields. deny.toml closed allowlist + crates.io-only ban unchanged; cargo-deny is release-only (NOT in the PR gate) so a stray dep would pass PR but break release -- do not add one.

crates/mcp/src stays free of Command::new/Command::spawn/TcpListener/UdpSocket (CI guard 1) and tokio::fs/std::fs/File::open/read_to_string/read_to_end (CI guard 2): the retry-gate reads a bool, the dedup nonce is a passthrough, command_stop forwards an IPC request -- the daemon owns every kill/spawn. Enforced every phase.

Both OS gates pass EVERY phase: wsl bash scripts/linux-gate.sh (fmt --check, clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings, nextest --workspace, TC47 load gate (load_noise_backpressure), MCP guards 1&2, rustc==1.95.0) AND pwsh -File scripts/windows-gate.ps1 (windows_no_console_spawn + windows_spawn_site_coverage each >=1). Every fix touches cfg(unix)/cfg(windows) spawn/kill/transport or tests, so single-OS testing is insufficient.

Trust axiom (governing design rule): NO returned error hides a side effect; NO caller -- nor the adapter retrying on its behalf -- is told to retry a non-idempotent op; EVERY returned state maps to caller intent. Degraded results carry the job_id + cursor + recover_hint, never a bare error once a job exists; the mutating-failure remedy says 'reconcile via command_status/runtime_state', never 'retry the tool'.

Every new MCP tool (command_stop, Phase 6) has at least one integration test exercising it THROUGH the daemon IPC end (TESTING.md 3.4: cargo nextest run -p terminal-commander-mcp --tests), not just a unit test of the handler.

Adding command_stop updates ALL atomic anchors in one commit (all VERIFIED at HEAD): 5 name lists (tools.rs ordered vec, tools.rs sorted vec with command_stop after command_status, mcp_stdio.rs sorted, mcp_live_daemon.rs sorted, system_discover.v1.json tools[]) + 3 count assertions (daemon_unavailable_envelope.rs assert_eq!(checked,36)->37 + its comment, catalogue_lists_thirty_seven->thirty_eight test name, mcp_live_daemon count string) + fixture map (live_tools 37->38, covered_live 33->34) + system_discover fixture + minimal_tool_args arm. Half-fix = CI red.

The new IPC method (CommandStop) keeps the 6+ sync surfaces mutually consistent: IpcRequest/IpcResponse enums + Params/Response structs, method_name() exhaustive match, DISCOVERABLE_METHODS, dispatch() arm, all_request_variants helper, re-exports (ipc/lib.rs, daemon ipc/mod.rs + lib.rs) -- or the system_discover_methods_match_dispatch parity test fails both directions.

Additive wire fields only: ProbeListEntry.tag/argv_head and CommandStartParams.dedup_nonce use #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if="Option::is_none")] -- non-breaking, no protocol version bump (matches the frames_suppressed/liveness precedent); old client ignores unknown, old daemon omits, new client sees None.

TC-4 argv is surfaced as a BOUNDED redacted head (argv[0..2] via the existing format_argv_metadata redaction), consistent with RISK_REGISTER R-06 (argv is operator-input-safe, already verbatim in command_exited summary) -- neither over- nor under-redacted.

TC-2 dedup NEVER silently collapses two legitimately-distinct rapid identical runs: nonce-preferred keying (distinct nonces => distinct jobs), a very short argv+cwd+tag fallback window, eviction on EVERY completion path (exit, cancel, spawn-failure at command.rs:548-560), and explicit never-collapse + never-block-legitimate-re-run tests.

TC-5 self_check NEVER false-REDs a healthy daemon: profile-gated to skip-or-assert-deny under read_only_observer / restrictive command_allow_roots / repo_only-no-root; reuses ONE cached immortal self-check bucket (no drop_bucket); bucket_count grows by exactly 1 over the daemon lifetime; a forced round-trip failure DOES yield failures>0 (negative test).

The LIVE machine daemon is NEVER restarted/killed by any build or test: all tests use their own TC_SOCKET + explicit data dir (AGENTS.md). The TC-2 dedup, TC-5 spawn round-trip, and TC-3 kill tests run against TEST sockets only.

TC-1b and TC-6 are co-designed in ONE wait-loop rewrite (Phase 3, shared body tools.rs:650-709): the start-arm error still returns Err (no job_id yet); only post-job_id errors become the degraded partial result. MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS stays 1000ms (no load-gate RPC-doubling risk).

Doc tool-count is authoritative ONLY in the live crates/mcp assertions (37 at HEAD -> 38 after command_stop); README/RELEASE_CHECKLIST (29) and TOOL_CONTROL_SURFACE.md (32) are stale -- reconcile only the gated anchors + the normative TOOL_CONTROL_SURFACE table, do not chase the non-gated stale references.

Governing MCP contract is docs/mcp/TOOL_CONTROL_SURFACE.md + docs/runtime/REALTIME_SIGNAL_CHANNEL.md (SPEC-13 subordinates SPEC-8 for the runtime contract): the run_and_watch timeout/degraded and command_stop doc changes land there with a SPEC-8/SPEC-13 amendment cross-reference -- do not edit the subordinate SPEC-8 in isolation.

Git: Conventional Commits (fix:/feat:), Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> footer, PR to a review branch off main; NO push/merge without explicit human approval (a fix:/feat: on main triggers release-please).

RISK_REGISTER rows are ADDED (R-07/R-08/R-09), never edited/removed; existing rows (incl. R-06) untouched; demoted to Resolved only after the fix lands WITH proof. BACKLOG gains rows for all six defects + the deferred items. Source-status discipline (TESTING.md 4) labels every new test live/partial/test-only/mock; 'unknown' is a hard commit fail.

No fake success (acceptance-gate ledger baked into every phase's verification): Phase 1 mutating-failure returns honest 'reconcile' remedy (no false 'retry'); Phase 2 dedup returns the REAL existing job_id; Phase 3 degraded result keeps the REAL job_id; Phase 4 into_parts threads the REAL tag; Phase 5 self_check returns failures>0 on REAL breakage; Phase 6 command_stop performs a REAL kill verified via runtime_state.
